macro_context.headline: Iran-truce optimism plus an Asian AI rally are lifting equities to records while a softer dollar and 4.42% 10Y leave gold/silver bid as the stagflation hedge.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR/USD pushed to 1.1736 as DXY broke 98.10 on Trump-Iran de-escalation talk. Risk-on sapped safe-dollar bid; euro caught the residual.
EURUSD.why: The dollar is selling on geopolitics, not yields — US10Y still 4.42%. That's a fragile rally for EUR until ECB delivers something hawkish on its own.
EURUSD.watch: ECB speakers across Europe today; any pushback on June cut pricing extends the move. 1.1790 caps; loss of 1.1700 hands it back to dollar bears next session.

headlines.title: Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
Eurozone inflation hits 3% as oil prices spike and economic growth slows
ECB, Bank of England Set to Hold Rates Unchanged Today

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: Cable cleared 1.3580 to 1.3588 on the broad dollar slide. Sterling shrugged off the BoE-on-hold narrative as risk-on did the lifting.
GBPUSD.why: GBP is trading like a high-beta euro right now — the Iran-truce risk bid is doing the work. Without UK-specific catalysts, this is a borrowed rally.
GBPUSD.watch: Watch the UK gilt curve; if 10Y gilts catch a bid alongside Treasuries, BoE cut odds rise and cable stalls. 1.3650 resistance, 1.3520 the give-back level.

headlines.title: The British Pound Is a Buy Now Over the U.S. Dollar as Iran War Continues
Hedge Funds Are Shorting the U.S. Dollar So Make This 1 Trade Now
FTSE 100 today: UK stocks gain, pound above $1.35 amid U.S.-Iran talks hopes

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: USD/JPY dropped to 156.37 as the dollar weakened broadly. Yen got a relief bid even with US10Y still elevated near 4.42%.
USDJPY.why: The pair is decoupling from yields short-term — risk-on usually hurts yen, but Iran de-escalation lifted JPY on safe-haven unwind from oil-importer angle.
USDJPY.watch: Tokyo CPI Friday is the next yen-mover. Below 154 opens 152; back above 158 means oil-shock fears returning and Iran optimism fading.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally
How Markets Reacted to Trump's Iran Post, in Charts
The Dollar Isn’t Done. Why It’s Outshining Rivals Amid Iran Crisis.

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: AUD/USD ripped to 0.7250 (+1.14%) — Asian risk rally plus weak dollar plus Samsung's $1T milestone fed the Aussie bid through 0.7240.
AUDUSD.why: AUD is the cleanest expression of the Iran-truce + AI-rally trade. China stimulus chatter is icing, but the move is global risk-on, not local fundamentals.
AUDUSD.watch: RBA minutes due; if any hawkish dissent surfaces, 0.7300 prints. China PMI later in the week is the bigger test — soft print kills this rally fast.

headlines.title: Dollar softens as Middle East ceasefire holds
Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?
The Next Trade You Should Make Comes from the Land Down Under

XAUUSD.name: Gold
XAUUSD.past: Gold ripped +$116.10 to 4,671.90 despite the risk-on tape. Stagflation pricing — Fed inflation gauge at 3.5% — beats geopolitics.
XAUUSD.why: Gold + risk assets up + dollar down + 10Y at 4.42% is the textbook stagflation trade. Real yields are sliding even as nominals stay sticky.
XAUUSD.watch: Watch US PCE and Fedspeak this week. A hot print extends the move toward 4,800; cooling inflation finally caps gold and rotates flows back into equities.

XAGUSD.name: Silver
XAGUSD.past: Silver detonated +4.26% to 76.22 — gold's leverage cousin caught the stagflation bid plus an industrial demand kicker from the AI capex cycle.
XAGUSD.why: Silver moves are amplified by the gold-silver ratio compression — and physical tightness chatter at LBMA is real, not just a meme.
XAGUSD.watch: If gold breaks 4,750, silver targets 80. The ratio sub-60 would mark a regime shift. Watch dealer premiums on coins as the leading tell.

Silver Standard Resources Q1 Earnings Call Highlights

USOIL.name: WTI Crude Oil
USOIL.past: WTI fell to 101.08 (-1.16%) as Trump telegraphed Iran-deal progress. The Hormuz risk premium that built last week is unwinding fast.
USOIL.why: Crude is a pure geopolitics trade right now — fundamentals (US inventories, OPEC compliance) are sidelined while the tape reacts headline-by-headline.
USOIL.watch: Any concrete US-Iran framework drops oil to 95; a reversal on Iranian counter-statement and 105 prints. EIA inventories Wednesday matter only at the margin.

headlines.title: Rally rolls on despite Iran tensions and economic divide

Gold and silver prices today, Tuesday, May 5: Lowest open for gold in over a month following attacks near Hormuz

US500.name: S&P 500
US500.past: S&P 500 closed at 7,259.2 (+0.81%) — a fresh record, less than 0.2% from its 52w high. AAPL did the heavy lifting, +2.66%.
US500.why: This is a melt-up driven by Iran-truce optimism and a renewed AI bid out of Asia. Market breadth has improved but is still tech-heavy.
US500.watch: PCE Friday is the binary risk. Hot print = volatility back above VIX 20 and a 2% pullback baseline; in-line and 7,300 breaks easily next session.

headlines.title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures rise as tech earnings continue to impress
Dow transports are nearing a bear market after erasing their entire April breakout: Chart of the Day
Resilience amid ruins: Why markets are hitting record highs despite the Iran war

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 reached 28,015.1 (+1.31%), record territory. Asian AI bid carried over — Samsung's $1T cap re-energized chip and platform names.
NAS100.why: The AI capex cycle just got reinforced — Asian cap leaders accelerating means hyperscaler infra spend re-rates upward. NVDA -1% is noise inside that.
NAS100.watch: AMD prints today — strong guide spreads to NVDA and ARM names; weak guide is the first credible bear catalyst since the rally started in February.

headlines.title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures rise as tech earnings continue to impress
Shopify’s slowing growth raises red flags for investors
S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 Post Record Highs on Tech Strength

US30.name: Dow Jones
US30.past: Dow added 0.73% to 49,298.2. Industrial and financial names participated as Treasury yields stayed stable and risk-on lifted cyclicals.
US30.why: Dow lagging Nasdaq again — the rotation isn't happening, AI-leadership is the only game. That's a narrow-market signal, not a healthy broadening.
US30.watch: If yields back up over 4.50% on the next CPI surprise, Dow gives back fastest because rate-sensitive value names dominate it. Watch 49,000 as the line.

headlines.title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures rise as tech earnings continue to impress
Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq notch fresh records as tech leads markets higher, oil falls
Dow Jones Futures Rise; Trump Hits Hormuz Pause; AMD, Lumentum, Arista Lead AI Earnings Movers

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: Bitcoin pushed back over 80,000 to 80,927 (+1.38%). Risk-on tape plus continued ETF inflows did the work; 82,000 is the next obvious target.
BTC.why: BTC dominance at 58.7% — alts aren't catching up yet despite ETH's bid. That tells you this is institutional bid, not retail euphoria — for now.
BTC.watch: If ETF flows print +$300M today, the 82K break comes intraday. A reversal under 79,500 with the tape strong elsewhere is the divergence tell.

headlines.title: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Tuesday, May 5, 2026: Bitcoin reaches over $81,000, hitting a high last seen in January
Why Bitcoin’s 20% Price Rally Reads Bearish Underneath
Coinbase CEO makes critical move before earnings

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: ETH up to 2,361 (+0.63%) — lagging BTC's bid as the dominance ratio creeps higher. The L2 fee narrative remains stuck at low-throughput levels.
ETH.why: ETH is in a structural rut versus BTC until Pectra impact translates to user activity, not just specs. Spot ETF flows are moderate, not surging.
ETH.watch: Watch the ETH/BTC cross — break below 0.029 means rotation continues and 2,200 prints. Reclaim 0.032 and ETH catches up to 2,500 fast.

headlines.title: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Tuesday, May 5, 2026: Bitcoin reaches over $81,000, hitting a high last seen in January
Kelp Blames LayerZero for $292 Million Hack, Plans Switch to Chainlink
How Solana Exchange Drift Plans to Repay Users After $295 Million Crypto Hack

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.past: NVDA slipped 1.00% to 196.50 despite the broad tech rally. Profit-taking ahead of AMD earnings, plus the 'no longer just NVDA' narrative gaining traction.
NVDA.why: NVDA isn't broken — it's de-rating relative to the basket as ASIC alternatives (AVGO, AMD MI400, hyperscaler in-house) start to take share at the margin.
NVDA.watch: AMD print tonight is the proxy. AMD beat = NVDA bid resumes; AMD miss reframes the chip trade and NVDA holds 195 as a floor for one more session.

AMD earnings on deck: Why we might see a stock sell-off

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: TSLA closed at 389.37 (-0.80%). EU FSD regulatory pushback offset robotaxi optimism; chart still 22% off the 52w high.
TSLA.why: TSLA's narrative whip continues — robotaxi vs regulator, energy vs auto-margin compression. Without a delivery surprise, it's a sideways grind.
TSLA.watch: Q2 deliveries data points start trickling out; any monthly run-rate beat versus consensus and 410 reclaims. Lose 380 and the chart targets 360.

Exclusive-Rivian mulls making its own lidar sensors, possibly in partnership with Chinese firms

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: AAPL surged 2.66% to 284.18 — back near 52w highs, single-handedly driving the S&P record close. Apple Intelligence rollout chatter resurfaced.
AAPL.why: AAPL is the safe-haven AI proxy — buyback yield + services growth + AI optionality without NVDA-level multiple risk. Macro de-risking flows here first.
AAPL.watch: WWDC scheduling/leaks are the next catalyst. A clear on-device-AI roadmap reclaims 290; without it, AAPL drifts back to 275 as the bid fades.

headlines.title: Micron stock is surging, hitting a new intraday record high. Why?

Intel Joined Elon Musk's Terafab. Will This Catalyst Cement the Stock's Turnaround?

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: MSFT slipped 0.54% to 411.38 — pause day after recent strength. Azure AI guidance comments from execs at conferences kept the longer-term thesis intact.
MSFT.why: MSFT is the cleanest hyperscaler AI play but the stock is range-bound until Azure growth re-accelerates above 30%. Compute supply is still the bottleneck.
MSFT.watch: Next datapoint is Build conference (later in the month) for AI tools cadence. Hold 405 = base case extends; below 400 invites profit-taking to 390.

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: META eased 0.89% to 604.96. Capex digestion plus regulatory chatter weighed; 24% off the 52w high keeps the stock in correction mode.
META.why: META's spend cycle is the tension — Reality Labs losses, AI capex ramp, but ad pricing data still solid. Market is voting with caution near-term.
META.watch: EU Digital Markets Act enforcement updates and ad-rev runrate from agency checks are the watch items. Reclaim 615 = trend repair; below 595 = 580 next.

headlines.title: Jobs data live updates: Layoffs tick up amid high-profile job cuts at Coinbase, Meta, and other workplaces
Magnificent Seven, Markets, and Mailbag with CEO Tom Gardner
Meta’s Texas AI Build And Space Solar Deal Reshape Investment View

GOOGL.name: Alphabet
GOOGL.past: Alphabet rose 1.35% to 388.43. Cloud growth and Gemini distribution metrics underpin the bid; near 52w highs again.
GOOGL.why: GOOGL is winning the AI-search defense — the worst-case 'ChatGPT eats Google' thesis isn't materializing in usage data, and Cloud is taking share.
GOOGL.watch: Watch search volume and YouTube ad pricing for the next leg. Reclaim 392 = uptrend resumes to 400; lose 382 and rotation worry resurfaces.

headlines.title: Tech stocks today: Intel pops, Semiconductor earnings in focus, Musk-Altman court battle continues
AI infrastructure: Is it time to trade in the Mag 7 for the Air 7?
Dow Jones Futures Rise; Trump Hits Hormuz Pause; AMD, Lumentum, Arista Lead AI Earnings Movers

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.past: AMZN added 0.55% to 273.55. AWS commentary continues to be the bull case; retail margins the slow-burn upside.
AMZN.why: AMZN's AWS growth is re-accelerating off the trough; the retail business is finally compounding margin gains rather than chasing top-line. Best two-engine setup.
AMZN.watch: Next AWS revenue update is the catalyst. Above 278 and the chart targets 285; loss of 269 invites a test of 260 as bot-flow profit-takes.
